question.text: Mark-to-market gains and losses on commodity futures positions are accounted for:

answer.text: On a daily basis in the books of accounts, reflecting the actual margin flows
Only at the end of the financial year
Only when the position is closed
Not required to be accounted for

explanation.text: **The Logic:** MTM gains/losses are booked daily as they represent actual cash flows (margin pay-in/pay-out). This matches the exchange's daily settlement process. **The Trap:** Not just year-end or on closure. Daily accounting reflects the actual margin movements. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series IX covers derivative accounting. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Daily MTM accounting provides transparent P&L tracking — it's essential for both tax reporting and management reporting of derivative positions.
